name: Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales
description: This moderate 27.5-mile mountain biking route circles the Embalse de Nogales, connecting historic towns in Extremadura, Spain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Salvatierra De Los Barros traverse a diverse landscape characterized by hilly terrain within the *serranía de Jerez de los Caballeros*. The region features a dehesa ecosystem with open woodlands of cork oaks and holm oaks, alongside streams and varied dirt tracks. This environment provides a mix of challenging ascents and descents, with technical sections and smoother paths. The natural features, including the Arroyo de Santa María and the unique "barreros," contribute to the distinct mountain biking experience. Best mountain bike trails around Salvatierra De Los Barros The most popular mountain bike trail is Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain, including sections around the Nogales Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop, a moderate 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route winds through the characteristic dehesa landscape, offering scenic views of the Extremadura countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra, a 32.7 miles (52.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the Villalba de los Barros Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Salvatierra De Los Barros is defined by its dehesa ecosystem, hilly terrain, and network of dirt tracks and technical sections. The routes offer options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging rides. The routes in Salvatierra De Los Barros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 120 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Salvatierra De Los Barros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Salvatierra De Los Barros. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from easy paths to more challenging rides, traversing the unique dehesa landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: The terrain around Salvatierra De Los Barros is characterized by hilly landscapes within the &lt;em&gt;serranía de Jerez de los Caballeros&lt;/em&gt;. You'll encounter a mix of dirt tracks, paths, and technical sections with ascents and descents. The region features a dehesa ecosystem with open woodlands of cork oaks and holm oaks, streams, and unique 'barreros' (clay pits) linked to the town's pottery tradition.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: Yes, Salvatierra De Los Barros offers 4 easy mountain bike trails, which are generally suitable for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous ride. Many of the 30 moderate trails also provide accessible options with varied terrain.

Q: What are the most challenging mountain bike trails in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 6 difficult mountain bike trails in Salvatierra De Los Barros. Routes like the 'Ruta de los Molinos' are known for their descents, ascents, and technical sections with loose stones, offering a more demanding experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Salvatierra De Los Barros are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359947973/salvatierra-a-salvaleon-a-nogales-circular-por-el-embalse-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales — circular por el Embalse de Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.3 km moderate route, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030784911/route-of-the-twelve-apostles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.0 km long and winds through the dehesa landscape.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: While mountain biking, you can encounter several notable natural features. The region is rich in dehesa ecosystems with ancient cork and holm oaks. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt;, a thousand-year-old olive tree, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks&lt;/a&gt;. The Arroyo de Santa María also offers refreshing landscapes with willows and ash trees.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Salvatierra De Los Barros offers several historical and cultural attractions. You can visit the 15th-century Salvatierra de los Barros Castle, the 16th-century Church of San Blas, or the Franciscan Convent of Santa María de Jesús. The town is also known for its pottery tradition, with a Museo de la Alfarería and Centro de Interpretación de la Alfarería.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with scenic views along the trails?
A: Yes, the hilly terrain and diverse landscape provide opportunities for scenic views. You can find viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding region and its characteristic dehesa ecosystem.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: The mountain bike trails in Salvatierra De Los Barros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. Over 120 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic dehesa landscapes and the mix of challenging and smoother paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Salvatierra De Los Barros is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the dehesa and hilly terrain.

Q: Is it possible to bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in natural areas like Salvatierra De Los Barros are dog-friendly, especially those traversing the dehesa landscape. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog under control, especially when passing through areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations if available.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Salvatierra De Los Barros?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of Salvatierra De Los Barros. For specific trailheads, it's often possible to find roadside parking or designated areas, particularly near popular starting points for routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.
tips: Salvatierra de los Barros is a municipality known internationally for what has been one of its economic pillars for centuries: pottery. Along with the ceramic industry, the breeding of the Iberian pig raised in freedom among holm oaks and cork oaks stands out.

Located between the Monsalud and Rozallana mountains, rolling along beautiful and picturesque trails combined with sigletracks, we are in the area of the route of the Twelve Apostles, planned by the Nature Classroom of the Popular University of Salvaleón and which owes its name to the existence of twelve hundred-year-old cork oaks (currently seven).

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Salvatierra de los Barros. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting point for the trail. Specific parking areas are usually available near the town center or main access roads.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It covers approximately 44.3 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 830 meters, requiring a good level of physical fitness. It's not recommended for absolute beginners but is suitable for riders with some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the route around Embalse de Nogales?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse landscape characteristic of Extremadura's Sierra Suroeste. The terrain includes "eternas dehesas" (eternal pastures) and "redondeadas sierras salpicadas aquí allá por rotundos riscos de piedra" (rounded mountains dotted with robust rock outcrops). The route also offers scenic views around the Embalse de Nogales, surrounded by gentle hills and olive groves.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Salvatierra a Salvaleón a Nogales route?
faqs.answer:
Beyond the natural beauty of the Embalse de Nogales and the dehesas, the route connects three historically significant towns:

  • Salvatierra de los Barros: Known for its artisan pottery and a 15th-century castle.
  • Salvaleón: An ancient village with dolmens, Roman villa remains, and the Monte Porrino cork oak forest.
  • Nogales: Home to a well-preserved 15th-century castle.

You will also pass by the Route of the Twelve Apostles (Centenary Cork Oaks).

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in Extremadura are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in towns. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and elevation, and always carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, no special permits or entrance fees are required to ride this mountain bike route. It traverses public and communal lands. However, always respect private property and local regulations, especially concerning natural areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this circular route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this route are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is typically milder, and the landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, making long rides challenging, while winters can be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects the towns of Salvatierra de los Barros, Salvaleón, and Nogales. These towns offer various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, where you can refuel and rest before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de Barcarrota a Nogales and the Cañada real.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), sun protection (sunscreen, hat), and appropriate cycling attire. A fully charged phone and a GPS device or the komoot app are also highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, distance of 44.3 km, and elevation gain of 830 m, this route is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy ride. It's better suited for families with older, experienced teenage riders who have good physical fitness.

waypoints.name: Route of the Twelve Apostles (Centenary Cork Oaks)
